zh.Bromley Bagot, Staffordshire,England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾布羅姆利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Hugh Bagot, Lord of Bromley於1196年出生在英國斯塔福爾巴格也斯 布羅姆利時，他的父親Sir Simon Bagot, Lord of Bromley19歲，母親of Blithfield21歲。他於大約1226年與Alicia Bigod在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1256年在他的家鄉享年60歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾巴格也斯 布羅姆利。

nameMeanings.name: Bagot,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Baggett   (see also   Baggott  ), from a pet form of   Bagg   2.

variants.name: Baggett
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Norman personal name Bagot, a diminutive of ancient Germanic Bago, from the root bag- ‘to fight’. Compare Bagg 2.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Bromley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places called Bromley, in Essex, Hertfordshire, Kent (now in Greater London), Greater London, Greater Manchester, Staffordshire, Shropshire, and elsewhere. Most are named with Old English brōm ‘broom’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’, but Bromley (near Bow) in Greater London is from Old English bræmbel ‘bramble’ + lēah.

variants.name: Brimley
meanings: habitational name, perhaps from Brimley in Devon or Brimbley in Stoke Abbott, Dorset, both named with Old English brōm ‘broom’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

variants.name: Brumley
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Bromley  . This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Hugh Bagot, Lord of Bromley was born in 1196, in Bagot's Bromley, Staffordshire, England, his father, Sir Simon Bagot, Lord of Bromley, was 19 and his mother, of Blithfield, was 21. He married Alicia Bigod about 1226, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died in 1256, in his hometown, at the age of 60, and was buried in Bagot's Bromley, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Hugh Bagot, Lord of Bromley est né en 1196, à Bagot's Bromley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Simon Bagot, Lord of Bromley, avait 19 ans et sa mère, of Blithfield, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Alicia Bigod environ 1226, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé en 1256, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Bagot's Bromley, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

Er hat um 1226 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Alicia Bigod geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb 1256 im Alter von 60 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Bagot's Bromley, Staffordshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Bagot, Lord of Bromleyが1196年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアバゴト'ス ブロムリーで生まれた時，父Sir Simon Bagot, Lord of Bromleyは19歳，母of Blithfieldは21歳であった。約1226年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアでAlicia Bigodと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。彼は1256年に故郷で60歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアバゴト'ス ブロムリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Bagot, Lord of Bromley(이)가 1196년 영국 스태퍼드실르 바고트'스 브롬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Simon Bagot, Lord of Bromley(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 of Blithfield(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1226년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 Alicia Bigod(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1256년 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 바고트'스 브롬리에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Alicia Bigod aproximadamente 1226, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Hugh Bagot, Lord of Bromley родился в 1196 г. в местности багот'с бромли, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Simon Bagot, Lord of Bromley было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали of Blithfield, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Alicia Bigod около 1226 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер в 1256 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности багот'с бромли, стаффордшир, Англия.

Se casó con Alicia Bigod aproximadamente 1226, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió en 1256, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en Bagot's Bromley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.
